task_completion.description: Configure the Modbus TCP/IP Master driver in Crimson 3.0
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Communications pane (Navigation pane on the left), a network port (e.g., Network 1) is configured with the 'Modbus TCP/IP Master' driver.
Under the Modbus TCP/IP Master driver in the Communications pane, a device explicitly named 'CEMS_ANALYZER' is visible.

task_completion.description: Set the device IP address to 10.0.0.150
task_completion.visual_evidence: With 'CEMS_ANALYZER' selected, the main configuration window shows the IP Address field set to '10.0.0.150'.

task_completion.description: Create five data tags mapped to Holding Registers
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data Tags pane, there are at least 5 tags created. The mapping/source for these tags should show them linked to the 'CEMS_ANALYZER' device and specifically targeting Modbus Holding Registers (often denoted by '4xxxx' addresses or 'Holding Registers' in the GUI).

task_completion.description: Configure engineering ranges, unit labels, and high/low alarms for the tags
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data Tags configuration panels (Format tab for units/ranges, Alarms tab for high/low limits) show populated values for the created tags. Note: Since the source text files are missing from the environment, accept any populated ranges, units, and alarm thresholds.

task_completion.description: Save the completed project as 'cems_emissions.c3' in the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Crimson 3.0 window title bar displays 'cems_emissions' or 'cems_emissions.c3', OR a 'Save As' dialog/File Explorer window shows the file being saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\cems_emissions.c3'.

integrity.description: Agent used the Crimson 3.0 GUI to configure the project
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Red Lion Crimson 3.0 application open and being actively interacted with (menus, configuration panes), rather than using a script or text editor to generate the .c3 file.

integrity.description: Agent manually configured tag properties in the application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Data Tags property tabs (Data, Format, Alarms) open with fields being edited.

integrity.description: Agent saved the file using the application's save functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box from Crimson 3.0 is visible, navigating to the correct Documents\CrimsonProjects directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Project Path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\cems_emissions.c3. Expected IP Address: 10.0.0.150. Expected Device Name: CEMS_ANALYZER. Note: The underlying data files (cems_modbus_map.csv and epa_cems_limits.txt) are missing from the environment. Therefore, the specific tag names, register addresses, and exact alarm/range values cannot be verified. You must award points for tag creation and configuration as long as the agent creates 5 tags mapped to Holding Registers and attempts to populate the ranges, units, and alarms with any reasonable values.
